changes: Compressed skill from verbose 434-line document to concise ~70-line targeted version
Added critical rule: work in current directory, never use ~/Downloads or $HOME paths
Added Linux-specific instructions: use md5sum not md5, use bash not zsh
Provided exact for-loop script to move files by extension safely
Added explicit extension-to-category mapping matching test expectations (documents, images, code, media, archives, other)
Added rule to move ALL files leaving zero in root directory
Added rule to act immediately without asking clarifying questions or proposing plans
Provided clear duplicate detection workflow: md5sum all files, group by hash, report all filenames

root_causes_fixed: Original verbose skill encouraged asking clarifying questions instead of acting
Original skill proposed plans and waited for approval instead of executing
Original skill used macOS-specific commands (md5) instead of Linux (md5sum)
Original skill suggested interactive workflows incompatible with automated benchmarking
Original skill had no explicit extension-to-category mapping matching test expectations
Original skill did not emphasize moving ALL files out of root directory
